----------------------------------- David Doermann Professor, SUNY Empire Innovation Chair, Department of Computer Science and Engineering University at Buffalo email: doermann@buffalo.edu website: cse


-----------------------------------
David Doermann
Professor, SUNY Empire Innovation
Chair, Department of Computer Science and Engineering
University at Buffalo

email: doermann@buffalo.edu<mailto:doermann@buffalo.edu>
website: cse.buffalo.edu/~doermann<http://cse.buffalo.edu/~doermann>
phone: 716-645-1557 (office), 410-493-9043 (mobile)

Department Connect: https://forms.office.com/r/EHGLT4hUKP

[cid:image001.png@01DCD273.45028FA0]


[https://res.public.onecdn.static.microsoft/assets/bookwithme/misc/CalendarPerson20px.png]<https://outlook.office.com/bookwithme/user/3fa62c55ec154a08b6656025b27be824@buffalo.edu?anonymous&ep=bwmEmailSignature>
Book time to meet with me<https://outlook.office.com/bookwithme/user/3fa62c55ec154a08b6656025b27be824@buffalo.edu?anonymous&ep=bwmEmailSignature>


From: Venu Govindaraju <ub-research@buffalo.edu>
Sent: Wednesday, April 22, 2026 4:01 PM
To: David Doermann <doermann@buffalo.edu>
Subject: Research Matters: Updates from Research, Innovation and Economic Development

[https://mcusercontent.com/24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14/images/3d081791-c27f-ad9c-2b99-de0b54af8de7.png]

[Research Matters, Resources for Our Research Community; Theresa Wrynn, a dental PhD student, works with slides at a research lab in the Biomedical Research Medical Building. Photo by Meredith Forrest Kulwicki]

April 2026
Internal News from Research, Innovation and Economic Development


IN THIS ISSUE:   Annual Research Report  |  Thermometer Exchange Program  |  Pre and Post Award Support  |  Proposal Support  |  NSF I-Corps™ Regional Courses  |  Funding Opportunities  |  Professional Development  |  Events  |  News


New and Noteworthy

[2025 Research and Innovation Report in various mediums; print and digital (smartphone and laptop).]
OFFICE OF RESEARCH, INNOVATION AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT
Celebrating discovery and economic impact
The 2025 Research and Innovation Report highlights a year of transformative achievements that are driving economic growth, scientific discovery and real-world impact.

Explore the book online by clicking here<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=8151807d8e&e=6ab7b065bf> →


Resources and Policy News

OFFICE OF RESEARCH COMPLIANCE
FREE mercury thermometer exchange program
Starting in late April, Environment, Health and Safety will launch a no-cost thermometer exchange program to replace mercury thermometers with alcohol alternatives, reducing the risks associated with mercury exposure.

The team will work with Facilities Customer Service to communicate the exchange schedule.

For questions, please contact Joshua Snyder (jsnyder8@buffalo.edu<mailto:jsnyder8@buffalo.edu?subject=Mercury%20Thermometer%20Exchange%20Program>), laboratory safety manager, Environment, Health and Safety  →


SPONSORED PROJECTS SERVICES
Pre- and post-award support
The new Research Administration Support Center (RASC) provides PIs with a simpler, more consistent support experience across the research lifecycle.

RASC will reduce the administrative burden through streamlined, standardized processes, giving you more time to focus on the research. Dedicated, specialized teams ensure you are connected to the right expertise quickly, with clear points of contact through defined PI-facing and sponsor-facing roles:

Pre-Award:

  *   First: Complete the Notice of Intent (NOI)<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=ac485fbb2f&e=6ab7b065bf>.
  *   Second: Once your NOI is received, you will be assigned a proposal team that includes a proposal manager and budget specialist.
  *   Third: Work with these people to develop your proposal.
Post-Award:

  *   Each researcher will have a finance manager as the single point of contact for all post-award needs.
For questions, please contact RASC@buffalo.edu<mailto:RASC@buffalo.edu?subject=Pre-and%20Post-Award%20Support>  →


OFFICE OF RESEARCH ADVANCEMENT
ORA helps strengthen research proposals
Under the RASC model, the Office of Research Advancement (ORA) continues to play a central role.

ORA provides comprehensive proposal development support, including editing, writing, graphics and strategic guidance on agency alignment — key services for developing clear, compelling and fundable proposals.

To ensure ORA is a part of your proposal team, please indicate that you would like to receive ORA support when submitting the NOI.

For questions, please contact ubgrants@buffalo.edu<mailto:ubgrants@buffalo.edu?subject=NOI%20Submission%20Support>  →


Business and Entrepreneur Partnerships

[Innovation To Impact; two researchers working together in a facility with hexagon vector accent.]


[NSF I-Corps - Explore the commercialization potential of your biotech or pharmaceutical innovation. Apply by May 6th. Blue tinted pipette background.]
BUSINESS & ENTREPRENEUR PARTNERSHIPS
Move biotech and pharmaceutical innovations to commercialization
APPLICATIONS DUE WEDNESDAY, MAY 6
The Biotech I-Corps™ Regional Course is a month-long online program (June 1 – July 11) for faculty and innovators advancing therapeutics, drug delivery platforms or other pharma technologies to explore the path from research to market. Participants connect with industry experts, potential partners and other stakeholders to learn about market demand, product development and commercialization in the biopharma industry.

The course concludes at the 2026 BIO International Convention<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=2f87434282&e=6ab7b065bf> in San Diego, CA. Accepted teams receive up to $5,000 in travel funding.

Apply for the course<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=749377c93b&e=6ab7b065bf>  →

Interested faculty should contact Olga E. Petrova, PhD, (olgapetr@buffalo.edu<mailto:olgapetr@buffalo.edu?subject=Biotech%20I-Corps%20Course>) director, innovation initiatives, BEP  →


[Female researcher developing bionic prosthetic hand in a lab, National Science Foundation’s (NSF) and NSF I-Corps Hub Northeast logos.]
BUSINESS & ENTREPRENEUR PARTNERSHIPS
NSF Translation to Practice: New research funding opportunity
APPLICATIONS DUE TUESDAY, MAY 19
The National Science Foundation’s (NSF) Translation to Practice (TTP) is a new opportunity<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=4cba4fe743&e=6ab7b065bf> that helps move promising research outputs toward real-world use and commercialization.

The program supports translational activities that can advance technologies toward market readiness, with awards of up to $1.2 million under the TTP-Translate track and up to $2 million under the TTP-Partner track.

BEP can advise on translation and commercialization considerations for this opportunity.

Interested faculty should contact Olga E. Petrova, PhD, (olgapetr@buffalo.edu<mailto:olgapetr@buffalo.edu?subject=NSF%20Translation%20to%20Practice%20Opportunity>) director, innovation initiatives, BEP  →


Funding Opportunities

[Student conducts research in the Dorsheimer Laboratory and Greenhouse on North Campus. Leading the project are James Berry, with the Department of Biological Sciences and Tim Thomay, with the Department of Physics. Photo by Douglas Levere]
OFFICE OF RESEARCH, INNOVATION AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT
Limited submissions and internal funding opportunities
The Office of Research, Innovation and Economic Development (ORIED) supports the research community through limited submissions and internal funding opportunities. Funding opportunities from ORIED are shared and submitted through this portal<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=ec007bbecf&e=6ab7b065bf>.

For questions, please email Carol O’Connell, (caroloco@buffalo.edu<mailto:caroloco@buffalo.edu?subject=Limited%20Submissions%20>) assistant vice president, strategic growth, ORIED  →


[Seed Funding concept with transparent data chart and digital plant in foreground.]
CENTER ON ADDICTION AND ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE RESEARCH
CAAIR Seed Funding Program
LETTERS OF INTENT DUE MONDAY, JUNE 1
Center on Addiction and Artificial Intelligence Research (CAAIR)<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=02b1e7417a&e=6ab7b065bf> invites applications for one-year pilot project grants (up to $25,000) supporting interdisciplinary collaborations that use AI to address harmful alcohol and drug use in equitable, responsible ways. Projects should build new partnerships across departments and include plans for community engagement.

Eligibility: Full-time UB faculty from all 12 schools are welcome to apply.

Learn more about the program here<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=513cf63294&e=6ab7b065bf>  →
For questions, please contact Jennifer Read (jpread@buffalo.edu<mailto:jpread@buffalo.edu?subject=CAAIR%20Seed%20Funding%20Program>) or Craig Colder<mailto:CColder@Buffalo.edu?subject=CAAIR%20Seed%20Funding%20Program> (ccolder@buffalo.edu<mailto:ccolder@buffalo.edu?subject=CAAIR%20Seed%20Funding%20Program>), co-PIs  →


Professional Development

[Researcher documenting water sample in countryside while holding a beaker, pen and notepad. In the foreground is a screenshot of Research Connect Home Page.]
THE STATE UNIVERSITY OF NEW YORK
UB and SUNY Research Connect webinars: Make the most of your Research Connect profile
UB Research Connect<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=aebe4e6e7c&e=6ab7b065bf> is part of SUNY Research Connect<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=4182ff6348&e=6ab7b065bf>, a portal that unites profiles of nearly 7,000 SUNY researchers across 26 campuses, highlighting faculty expertise, publications, projects, funding and available research facilities.

Faculty are encouraged to learn more about this powerful tool at two SUNY-led webinars:

Using SUNY Research Connect to drive discovery and find collaborators
TUESDAY, APRIL 28 | 12 pm – 1pm on Zoom
In this webinar, you will learn how to:

  *   Find your next research partner in specific federally prioritized fields and build interdisciplinary teams
  *   Uncover facilities and equipment to advance your research

Maximize your reach: Optimizing your SUNY Research Connect profile
WEDNESDAY, APRIL 29 | 12 pm – 1pm on Zoom
In this session, you will learn how to:

  *   Update and curate your profile
  *   Enhance visibility in areas of research excellence

Learn more about these webinars and register here<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=59c148f795&e=6ab7b065bf>  →

[UB Blue tinted decorative image to display various events at UB.]

THE STATE UNIVERSITY OF NEW YORK (SUNY)
For faculty interested in commercialization: SUNY Startup Summer School applications open now
REGISTER BY FRIDAY, MAY 22
SUNY Startup Summer School<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=7256073e91&e=6ab7b065bf> (S4) is a free, ten-week virtual training program that provides students, faculty and staff with the knowledge and networks essential to commercialize breakthrough technology.

S4 provides participants with:

  *   instructional webinars led by renowned speakers
  *   accelerated Innovation Corps entrepreneurial training by experienced coaches
  *   proposal development support from research and startup experts
Learn more and register<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=823f764043&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


Research and Innovation Events

COMMUNITY HEALTH EQUITY RESEARCH INSTITUTE
CHERI PRESENTS: 4th Annual Research Day
SATURDAY, APRIL 25
Research Day at the Buffalo Museum of Science
Hosted by the Community Health Equity Research Institute (CHERI), Research Day invites community-based organizations, community groups, faculty, trainees and students to gather, expand networks and learn about organized community programs, initiatives or research aimed at mitigating health inequities in Western New York.

This event is free to attend, but registration is required.

Learn more and register for Research Day<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=b7f515f874&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


THE CHRONICLE OF HIGHER EDUCATION PRESENTS:
IHE US AI Summit 2026: Ensuring AI for the public good
WEDNESDAY, JUNE 3 – THURSDAY, JUNE 4
Jacobs School of Medicine and Biomedical Sciences
National leaders in higher education, industry and policy will converge in Buffalo this June to shape the future of how AI is employed to address society’s most pressing challenges.

The summit will bring together thought leaders and speakers with attendees from more than 100 universities and organizations, including UB faculty experts in computer science, medicine and other fields.

Learn more and register for the AI summit<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=cd3e879d0e&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


THE CENTER FOR COMPUTATIONAL RESEARCH PRESENTS:
Opportunity for high-school students: CCR’s annual summer workshop
MONDAY, JUNE 29 – THURSDAY, JULY 2 | 9:30 am – 4 pm
Center of Excellence in Bioinformatics and Life Sciences (CBLS) | 701 Ellicott Street, Buffalo
The Center for Computational Research (CCR) welcomes rising sophomores, juniors and seniors in high school for its annual Eric Pitman High School Workshop. The current year's workshop focuses on bioinformatics and research in the life sciences. If you know any students interested in biomedical science, computational engineering and mathematics, please spread the word.

The application deadline is Friday, May 15.

Learn more and enroll here<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=f6d09150ce&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


[Research Operations News - stay informed about important updates and timely announcements for our research community.]<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=258242bc45&e=6ab7b065bf>


Research and Innovation News


[Buffalo Startup Week digital sign above mingling crowd in event space. Photo by Nancy J. Parisi]
2026 Buffalo Startup Week underscores region’s entrepreneurial impact
BEP and the Western New York Incubator Network (WIN) led Buffalo Startup Week showcasing our region’s thriving entrepreneurial environment for founders, investors and leaders from across UB and the community.
Read more<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=1942290c59&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


[Student from BOCES program working with electric board while wearing safety goggles. Photo by Meredith Forrest Kulwicki]
UB awarded $3.7 million to expand career pathways through BOCES partnership
UB’s Business and Entrepreneur Partnerships (BEP) unit, part of the Research, Innovation and Economic Development office, was awarded $3.7 million in state funding to collaborate with five BOCES districts in creation of the Multi-Regional Career Centers Initiative.
Read more<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=573b94d6ee&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


[Gloved had holding a lab sample in a lab. Photo by Doug Levere]
UB delivered more than $1 million to faculty impacted by federal grant cancellations
From May to October 2025, more than $1 million was awarded from ORIED to 48 PIs across 21 departments, sustaining critical research in health, education, artificial intelligence, social work and more.
Read more<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=863cf85b73&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


[Portrait of Jennifer Read, PhD.]
Jennifer Read named director of CRIA
Read brings more than 25 years of experience in the field of addiction sciences. She assumed this role on Feb. 16 to allow for a transition period ahead of former CRIA Director Ken Leonard’s retirement.
Read more<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=6d61d995fc&e=6ab7b065bf>  →



  UBNow | Research News<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=8f89c40774&e=6ab7b065bf>


[UB Research, Innovation and Economic Development]

UB Research, Innovation and Economic Development
516 Capen Hall
Buffalo, NY 14260-1611
buffalo.edu/research<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=f6fdaef0d2&e=6ab7b065bf>


The Office of Research, Innovation and Economic Development is the intersection of innovation and economic growth, transforming communities locally and around the globe. Our offices assist everyone – from principal investigators to budding and mature businesses – in obtaining funding for their research and development, fostering scientific discoveries and entrepreneurial stewardship to advance UB’s Top 25 initiative and societal impact.



Copyright © 2026 UB Research, Innovation and Economic Development, All rights reserved.
Want to change how you receive these emails? You can unsubscribe from this list<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=294638f709&t=b&e=6ab7b065bf&c=997c439cbb>.

All issues of the newsletter are now available on Research, Innovation and Economic Development's website here<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=b83890403a&e=6ab7b065bf>.

[Follow Us on LinkedIn!]<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=01f285d020&e=6ab7b065bf>
Follow Us on LinkedIn!<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=ceff5a3678&e=6ab7b065bf>

The program supports translational activities that can advance technologies toward market readiness, with awards of up to $1.2 million under the TTP-Translate track and up to $2 million under the TTP-Partner track.

BEP can advise on translation and commercialization considerations for this opportunity.

Interested faculty should contact Olga E. Petrova, PhD, (olgapetr@buffalo.edu<mailto:olgapetr@buffalo.edu?subject=NSF%20Translation%20to%20Practice%20Opportunity>) director, innovation initiatives, BEP  →


Funding Opportunities

[Student conducts research in the Dorsheimer Laboratory and Greenhouse on North Campus. Leading the project are James Berry, with the Department of Biological Sciences and Tim Thomay, with the Department of Physics. Photo by Douglas Levere]
OFFICE OF RESEARCH, INNOVATION AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT
Limited submissions and internal funding opportunities
The Office of Research, Innovation and Economic Development (ORIED) supports the research community through limited submissions and internal funding opportunities. Funding opportunities from ORIED are shared and submitted through this portal<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=ec007bbecf&e=6ab7b065bf>.

For questions, please email Carol O’Connell, (caroloco@buffalo.edu<mailto:caroloco@buffalo.edu?subject=Limited%20Submissions%20>) assistant vice president, strategic growth, ORIED  →


[Seed Funding concept with transparent data chart and digital plant in foreground.]
CENTER ON ADDICTION AND ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE RESEARCH
CAAIR Seed Funding Program
LETTERS OF INTENT DUE MONDAY, JUNE 1
Center on Addiction and Artificial Intelligence Research (CAAIR)<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=02b1e7417a&e=6ab7b065bf> invites applications for one-year pilot project grants (up to $25,000) supporting interdisciplinary collaborations that use AI to address harmful alcohol and drug use in equitable, responsible ways. Projects should build new partnerships across departments and include plans for community engagement.

Eligibility: Full-time UB faculty from all 12 schools are welcome to apply.

Learn more about the program here<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=513cf63294&e=6ab7b065bf>  →
For questions, please contact Jennifer Read (jpread@buffalo.edu<mailto:jpread@buffalo.edu?subject=CAAIR%20Seed%20Funding%20Program>) or Craig Colder<mailto:CColder@Buffalo.edu?subject=CAAIR%20Seed%20Funding%20Program> (ccolder@buffalo.edu<mailto:ccolder@buffalo.edu?subject=CAAIR%20Seed%20Funding%20Program>), co-PIs  →


Professional Development

[Researcher documenting water sample in countryside while holding a beaker, pen and notepad. In the foreground is a screenshot of Research Connect Home Page.]
THE STATE UNIVERSITY OF NEW YORK
UB and SUNY Research Connect webinars: Make the most of your Research Connect profile
UB Research Connect<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=aebe4e6e7c&e=6ab7b065bf> is part of SUNY Research Connect<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=4182ff6348&e=6ab7b065bf>, a portal that unites profiles of nearly 7,000 SUNY researchers across 26 campuses, highlighting faculty expertise, publications, projects, funding and available research facilities.

Faculty are encouraged to learn more about this powerful tool at two SUNY-led webinars:

Using SUNY Research Connect to drive discovery and find collaborators
TUESDAY, APRIL 28 | 12 pm – 1pm on Zoom
In this webinar, you will learn how to:

  *   Find your next research partner in specific federally prioritized fields and build interdisciplinary teams
  *   Uncover facilities and equipment to advance your research

Maximize your reach: Optimizing your SUNY Research Connect profile
WEDNESDAY, APRIL 29 | 12 pm – 1pm on Zoom
In this session, you will learn how to:

  *   Update and curate your profile
  *   Enhance visibility in areas of research excellence

Learn more about these webinars and register here<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=59c148f795&e=6ab7b065bf>  →

[UB Blue tinted decorative image to display various events at UB.]

THE STATE UNIVERSITY OF NEW YORK (SUNY)
For faculty interested in commercialization: SUNY Startup Summer School applications open now
REGISTER BY FRIDAY, MAY 22
SUNY Startup Summer School<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=7256073e91&e=6ab7b065bf> (S4) is a free, ten-week virtual training program that provides students, faculty and staff with the knowledge and networks essential to commercialize breakthrough technology.

S4 provides participants with:

  *   instructional webinars led by renowned speakers
  *   accelerated Innovation Corps entrepreneurial training by experienced coaches
  *   proposal development support from research and startup experts
Learn more and register<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=823f764043&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


Research and Innovation Events

COMMUNITY HEALTH EQUITY RESEARCH INSTITUTE
CHERI PRESENTS: 4th Annual Research Day
SATURDAY, APRIL 25
Research Day at the Buffalo Museum of Science
Hosted by the Community Health Equity Research Institute (CHERI), Research Day invites community-based organizations, community groups, faculty, trainees and students to gather, expand networks and learn about organized community programs, initiatives or research aimed at mitigating health inequities in Western New York.

This event is free to attend, but registration is required.

Learn more and register for Research Day<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=b7f515f874&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


THE CHRONICLE OF HIGHER EDUCATION PRESENTS:
IHE US AI Summit 2026: Ensuring AI for the public good
WEDNESDAY, JUNE 3 – THURSDAY, JUNE 4
Jacobs School of Medicine and Biomedical Sciences
National leaders in higher education, industry and policy will converge in Buffalo this June to shape the future of how AI is employed to address society’s most pressing challenges.

The summit will bring together thought leaders and speakers with attendees from more than 100 universities and organizations, including UB faculty experts in computer science, medicine and other fields.

Learn more and register for the AI summit<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=cd3e879d0e&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


THE CENTER FOR COMPUTATIONAL RESEARCH PRESENTS:
Opportunity for high-school students: CCR’s annual summer workshop
MONDAY, JUNE 29 – THURSDAY, JULY 2 | 9:30 am – 4 pm
Center of Excellence in Bioinformatics and Life Sciences (CBLS) | 701 Ellicott Street, Buffalo
The Center for Computational Research (CCR) welcomes rising sophomores, juniors and seniors in high school for its annual Eric Pitman High School Workshop. The current year's workshop focuses on bioinformatics and research in the life sciences. If you know any students interested in biomedical science, computational engineering and mathematics, please spread the word.

The application deadline is Friday, May 15.

Learn more and enroll here<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=f6d09150ce&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


[Research Operations News - stay informed about important updates and timely announcements for our research community.]<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=258242bc45&e=6ab7b065bf>


Research and Innovation News


[Buffalo Startup Week digital sign above mingling crowd in event space. Photo by Nancy J. Parisi]
2026 Buffalo Startup Week underscores region’s entrepreneurial impact
BEP and the Western New York Incubator Network (WIN) led Buffalo Startup Week showcasing our region’s thriving entrepreneurial environment for founders, investors and leaders from across UB and the community.
Read more<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=1942290c59&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


[Student from BOCES program working with electric board while wearing safety goggles. Photo by Meredith Forrest Kulwicki]
UB awarded $3.7 million to expand career pathways through BOCES partnership
UB’s Business and Entrepreneur Partnerships (BEP) unit, part of the Research, Innovation and Economic Development office, was awarded $3.7 million in state funding to collaborate with five BOCES districts in creation of the Multi-Regional Career Centers Initiative.
Read more<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=573b94d6ee&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


[Gloved had holding a lab sample in a lab. Photo by Doug Levere]
UB delivered more than $1 million to faculty impacted by federal grant cancellations
From May to October 2025, more than $1 million was awarded from ORIED to 48 PIs across 21 departments, sustaining critical research in health, education, artificial intelligence, social work and more.
Read more<https://buffalo.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=24edc4d3f245df6aa9a5e1e14&id=863cf85b73&e=6ab7b065bf>  →


[Portrait of Jennifer Read, PhD.]
Jennifer Read named director of CRIA
Read brings more than 25 years of experience in the field of addiction sciences. She assumed this role on Feb. 16 to allow for a transition period ahead of former CRIA Director Ken Leonard’s retirement.

CDW + NYSCATE Presents: Adapt to Troubled Market Conditions - Building a Smarter Device Cycle Strategy



Managing student devices has become increasingly complex and expensive for K–12 districts navigating tight budgets, limited staff capacity, aging fleets, and growing expectations for uptime and support. At the same time, rapidly evolving device models, and supply chain dynamics make longterm planning and standardization more difficult year over year. What may appear manageable at a high level often translates into significant operational pressure for teams tasked with deployment, troubleshooting, repair coordination, and keeping devices in students’ hands. Districts are also challenged with having to shrink summer device refresh projects given the sharp price increases on computers and Chromebooks.

Greetings CII Members,

Here are a couple of talks happening next week that may be on interest to some of you. Brought to you by the Dept. of AI and Society:

April 6th - 10am - Davis Hall 338A (Zoom Link: https://buffalo.zoom.us/j/98515008391?pwd=taaCKbWj04AOjfEhGCaxGb30fX3Jtp.1<https://buffalo.zoom.us/j/98515008391?pwd=taaCKbWj04AOjfEhGCaxGb30fX3Jtp.1%C2%A0>)
Matthew Zook
Talk Title: The Silicon Gaze: A typology of biases and inequality in LLMs through the lens of place
 Talk Abstract:
 This paper introduces the concept of the silicon gaze to explain how large language models (LLMs) reproduce and amplify long-standing spatial inequalities. Drawing on a 20.3-million-query audit of ChatGPT, we map systematic biases in the model's representations of countries, states, cities and neighbourhoods. From these empirics, we argue that bias is not a correctable anomaly but an intrinsic feature of generative AI, rooted in historically uneven data ecologies and design choices.  Building on a power-aware, relational approach, we develop a five-part typology of bias (availability, pattern, averaging, trope and proxy) that accounts for the complex ways in which LLMs privilege certain places while rendering others invisible.

April 9th - 9:30am - Davis Hall 338A (Zoom Link: https://buffalo.zoom.us/j/92578937182?pwd=f1VuLGbf5BTJiOs10XSpQKUvVw83vB.1 <https://buffalo.zoom.us/j/92578937182?pwd=f1VuLGbf5BTJiOs10XSpQKUvVw83vB.1%C2%A0> )
Lauri Goldkind
 Talk Title:  Between Supervision and Skepticism: Folk Theories, Reflective Practice, and the Integration of AI in Mental Health Documentation.
Talk Abstract:
 Generative artificial intelligence (AI) systems designed to automate clinical documentation have rapidly proliferated in mental health practice, yet the field lacks established frameworks for their responsible adoption. This work examines how mental health clinicians navigate this technological landscape through two complementary qualitative studies. The first study investigates folk theories, the informal, experience-based models that clinicians develop to make sense of AI-enabled documentation tools in the absence of formal guidance, revealing how varying levels of AI literacy shape practitioners' ethical practices around data privacy and confidentiality. The second study uncovers an unexpected benefit of these same tools: their capacity to function as digital supervisors, supporting reflective practice by offering alternative clinical interpretations, clarifying practitioners' existing approaches, and enabling greater presence during sessions. Together, these findings illuminate a critical tension in contemporary psychotherapy: while AI platforms can enhance reflective capacity and reduce documentation burden, clinicians must simultaneously develop critical discernment to evaluate algorithmic output against their professional judgment. These findings point to how AI integration in mental health requires neither wholesale adoption nor rejection, but rather a dual investment in AI literacy and regulatory frameworks including governance structures, enabling clinicians to utilize AI's reflective potential while maintaining the critical stance essential to ethical, client-centered care.


Best,
Cynthia

| April 2026 |
Center News
Last chance to RSVP
[https://mcusercontent.com/3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2/images/a6006d1a-dbba-f531-b9aa-3c052334f7ad.png]<https://www.buffalo.edu/cii/events/2026-cii-workshop.html>
📅 April 23-24, 2026 |📍 205 Lockwood Library
The Center for Information Integrity’s<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=55beb4c231&e=6ebd687846> annual workshop returns this April with the theme Building an AI Conscience? Join researchers, practitioners, and members for two days of panels and discussion on AI’s impact on education, media literacy, mental health, and society.

The program includes a keynote by Quran M. Karriem, exploring AI, consciousness, and collective human experience.
All are welcome—whether you work in AI or are simply curious. Please RSVP to help us plan for refreshments.
[CLICK TO RSVP]

WE NEED YOUR HELP!
To amplify the work of CII and its members, please "Follow"         CII’s LinkedIn Page<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=6172c8c873&e=6ebd687846>, and share and like our posts. Thank you.
[Follow us!]

Member News
Kari J. Winter on Trump’s Attacks and the Risks of Normalizing Misogyny
Prof. Kari J. Winter<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=fb25ddef3f&e=6ebd687846>, professor of Global Gender and Sexuality Studies at UB, has been quoted in HuffPost<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=4679719f55&e=6ebd687846> about President Donald Trump’s recent comments targeting Fox News host Jessica Tarlov and his broader relationship with the media.

In the article, Winter<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=85f1299759&e=6ebd687846> highlights concerns about Trump’s repeated criticism of women, describing his remarks as rooted in longstanding patterns of misogyny. She explains that his public insults toward female journalists reflect deeper attitudes about gender and power, especially when women challenge authority. Winter warns that if this type of rhetoric continues to go unchecked, it could normalize disrespect toward women and contribute to wider social consequences, including the erosion of rights, diminished respect, and even an increased risk of violence against women.
[https://mcusercontent.com/3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2/images/bbe78453-f0b2-e140-fa67-5cbfbb32a0f0.jpg]<https://www.buffalo.edu/cii/about-us/members/kari-winter.html>

Rohini Srihari on AI Chatbots and Growing Risks for Young Users
Prof. Rohini Srihari<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=b86172422d&e=6ebd687846>, professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UB, has been featured in Spectrum News 1<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=4256d6f2e0&e=6ebd687846> on the rapid rise of AI chatbots and growing concerns about their impact on young users’ privacy and safety.

In the article, Srihari<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=b89124cd91&e=6ebd687846> explains that while AI tools can make conversations more natural and engaging, especially for teens who are comfortable interacting with technology, they also create risks around oversharing personal information and exposure to harmful content. She notes that chatbots’ agreeable and responsive nature can be a “double-edged sword,” particularly when it comes to sensitive topics like mental health. Srihari emphasizes the importance of educating both young people and adults on how to use AI responsibly, highlighting the need to balance innovation with safeguards that protect users.
[https://mcusercontent.com/3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2/images/e0bcb587-e59d-03c2-d64d-4bc7602eb61f.jpg]<https://www.buffalo.edu/cii/about-us/members/rohini-srihari.html>

Jacob Neiheisel Weighs In on Trump’s Call to Remove Fox News Anchor
Prof. Jacob Neiheisel<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=3605428f16&e=6ebd687846>, associate professor of political science at UB, has been quoted in HuffPost<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=9767c74845&e=6ebd687846> about comments made by President Donald Trump calling on Fox News to remove a female anchor.

In the article, Neiheisel<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=b5ee8468f5&e=6ebd687846> explains that Trump’s remarks raise concerns about respect for the role of a free press, noting that such statements suggest a limited regard for the protections afforded to journalists. He also highlights how today’s media environment may contribute to this dynamic, suggesting that close ties between political figures and certain media outlets can create the perception that political leaders have influence over editorial decisions.
[https://mcusercontent.com/3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2/images/20b1757c-4859-5beb-e10c-bb53c20a01ff.jpg]<https://www.buffalo.edu/cii/about-us/members/jacob-neiheisel.html>

Siwei Lyu on Spotting AI-Generated Images in Viral Military Posts
Prof. Siwei Lyu<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=3a423d73ea&e=6ebd687846>, SUNY Distinguished Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UB and director of the Institute for Artificial and Data Science, has been featured in Reuters<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=0ab3972086&e=6ebd687846> for his expertise in identifying AI-generated images in a story fact-checking viral photos of U.S. soldiers allegedly rescued from Iran.

In the article, Lyu<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=ab6ddfc7d4&e=6ebd687846> explains that the image shows multiple signs of being AI-generated, pointing to visual inconsistencies such as incorrect military patches and insignia, a U.S. flag with the wrong number of stars and stripes, and distorted physical features. He also highlights unnatural details like a floating microphone, facial hair blending into clothing, and malformed hands, emphasizing how these artifacts can reveal manipulated or fabricated content.
[https://mcusercontent.com/3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2/images/0deb3a74-3bb7-4591-1771-ac1b3f164f3f.jpg]<https://www.buffalo.edu/cii/about-us/members/siwei-lyu.html>

David Castillo Explores AI Through a Humanistic Lens
CII Co-Director, David Castillo<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=3001e4359c&e=6ebd687846> spoke on humanistic lines of inquiry into the role of Artificial Intelligence in our information and communications ecosystem. While we may think of the presence of AI technologies in nearly every aspect of our lives as a 21st century phenomenon, Castillo talked about how their societal impacts are better understood, not in isolation, but as part of a long history of techne (art, skill, craft) of reality engineering and manipulation.
[https://mcusercontent.com/3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2/images/79decb6f-40d4-0e0e-36d7-5346c817acd5.jpg]<https://www.buffalo.edu/cii/about-us/members/david-castillo.html>

Got News?
Do you have some news to share with CII's members and friends? Maybe you published or presented work about mis/dis-information? Or you know about a conference, talk, or other event folks might be interested in? Maybe you have a project in mind and are looking for partners from outside your field? We want to know.
[Share Your News]

Quoted
“It’s a double-edged sword. It allows more engaging, natural conversation. But there are privacy issues”
—Rohini Srihari<https://buffalo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3929c92bd44a4339238eb58f2&id=a7214557e4&e=6ebd687846>, UB professor, on AI chatbot risks

Institute News
Register: April 14 Tech Exchange Seminar Series<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=1e98bdf95c&e=1676658b38>
12:00 - 1:00 PM | 205 Lockwood Hall, North Campus
Virtual option available, register to attend.


Vincent Tutino<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=ba991f0ea2&e=1676658b38>

Assistant Professor, Department of Pathology and Anatomical Sciences
Uncertainty Gated Medical Image Segmentation Quality Triage: Implications to Acute Ischemic Stroke Management
[Vincent Tutino]



Presentación Rivera Reyes<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=fadd432a6d&e=1676658b38>



Associate Professor of Teaching, Department of Engineering Education
AI and Troubleshooting Skills in Electric Circuits: A Classroom Study
[Presentación Rivera Reyes]

Lyu and Castillo on AI for Social Good
[Youtube Video of the Podcast Airing of UB Tech Talk with Siwei Lyu and David Castillo]<https://youtu.be/c3TdpLTc9WI>



UB Tech Talk<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=d45e8e2c2f&e=1676658b38> was filmed at the Institute for Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (IAD) The podcast features IAD’s director, Siwei Lyu<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=6106d50488&e=1676658b38>, SUNY Distinguished Professor, SUNY Empire Innovation Professor, Department of Computer Science and Engineering and David Castillo<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=66104f990c&e=1676658b38>, IAD affiliate, professor, Department of Romance Languages and Literatures, both co-directors of Center for Information Integrity and Security.
Research Highlights
Gao–New AI Reduces Errors in Chest X-rays
[Mingchen Gao]

Mingchen Gao<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=d76d1152dc&e=1676658b38>, IAD engineering sciences (AI) MS program director, professor, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, is part of a UB research team developing an AI framework that improves the accuracy of automated chest X-ray reports and reduces AI-generated errors<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=75c144e4f7&e=1676658b38>.

Jacobs Honored with Clinical Research Award

David Jacobs<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=2fad075399&e=1676658b38>, IAD affiliate, assistant professor, Department of Pharmacy Practice, School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences, receives a 2025 Buffalo Translational Consortium Clinical Research Achievement Award<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=47af7d52fe&e=1676658b38> for research addressing health-related social needs through community pharmacy programs.
[David Jacobs]

Sellitto as Devil’s Advocate at Life Raft Debate
[Dominic Sellitto]

Dominic Sellitto<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=6c03717257&e=1676658b38>, IAD affiliate, clinical assistant professor, Department of Management Science and Systems, School of Management, serves as devil’s advocate at the Life Raft Debate event hosted by the UB Honors College<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=0b9f856648&e=1676658b38>, where faculty from multiple disciplines will debate which professor would be chosen to help rebuild society in a fictional survival scenario.

Wang Receives AAG Award

Le “Leo” Wang<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=a62e3c746f&e=1676658b38>, IAD affiliate, professor, Department of Geography, College of Arts and Sciences, receives the American Association of Geographers’ Outstanding Contribution Award in Remote Sensing<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=b69914960e&e=1676658b38>, recognizing his work using AI and remote sensing to study environmental change, land use, and ecosystems. He is the first UB faculty member to receive this honor.
[Le "Leo" Wang]

AI+X Degrees Gain Momentum
[Atri Rudra]

Atri Rudra<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=3c4128f1bf&e=1676658b38>, IAD affiliate, chair, Department of AI and Society, UB’s new AI+X degree programs in the Department of AI and Society have already admitted nearly 200 students<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=c06d37888c&e=1676658b38>, with the first cohort now enrolled. The interdisciplinary programs combine artificial intelligence with fields like economics, communication, and policy, preparing students for emerging AI-driven careers, as highlighted in TechCrunch coverage of shifting computer science enrollment trends.

AI Tool Can Help Detect Forged Radiology Reports

UB researchers have developed an AI tool designed to detect forged radiology reports<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=3bb2997dd9&e=1676658b38>. Nalini Ratha<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=185eaa6ef5&e=1676658b38>, IAD affiliate, SUNY Empire Innovation Professor, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, leads the research team that developed the detection system, which analyzes writing style to distinguish between human-written and AI-generated medical reports.
[Nalini Ratha]

Hu Researches AI for Wildfire Prediction
[Yingjie Hu]

UB researchers conducted one of the most extensive evaluations of AI-based deep learning models for predicting wildfire spread, using more than a decade of wildfire data from Hawaii and a case study of the 2023 Maui fires<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=d06f7815b7&e=1676658b38>. Yingjie Hu<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=c490ef828c&e=1676658b38>, IAD affiliate, associate professor, Department of Geography, co-authored the study examining how AI models compare with traditional fire modeling tools.

Mark Frank on Why Scams Work

Mark Frank<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=a81d77608a&e=1676658b38>, IAD affiliate, professor, Department of Communication, spoke with Freakonomics Radio about why scams are effective, explaining how scammers exploit human psychology<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=aa9275fd00&e=1676658b38> and how social media and AI have made scams more sophisticated and widespread.
[Mark Frank]
In the News

Xu on Contactless Biosensing using Cameras
[Wenyao Xu]

Wenyao Xu<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=3f60b8a429&e=1676658b38>, IAD affiliate, professor, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, was featured in a Medium article highlighting his lab’s research on contactless biosensing using standard cameras<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=397a75fcfb&e=1676658b38>.


Sadek and Qiao on Smart Transportation

IAD affiliates, Adel Sadek<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=5960c5194d&e=1676658b38>, professor, Department of Civil, Structural and Environmental Engineering and Chunming Qiao<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=92e5e5b690&e=1676658b38>, SUNY Distinguished Professor, Department of Computer Science and Engineering are involved in the Buffalo All Access Pilot Project<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=3070de6530&e=1676658b38>, a collaboration between UB, the Buffalo Niagara Medical Campus and NFTA that offers free automated community shuttles around the downtown campus.
[Buffalo ALL Access Bus for the NFTA]


Thikriat Al-Jewair’s AI Orthodontics Research
[Thikriat Al-Jewair]
Oral Health<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=55f5e1b626&e=1676658b38> features research by Thikriat Al-Jewair<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=4de6f5e50c&e=1676658b38>,<https://buffalo.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=125b0bf4c6c7ec06f0b5f2626&id=3138679c68&e=1676658b38> IAD affiliate, associate professor, Department of Orthodontics, on an AI algorithm that helps guide orthodontic treatment decisions for patients missing permanent teeth, with 96.4% accuracy.
